title: Bankroll-aware position sizing — lower max to $50, replace fixed base with bankroll percentage
objective: Replace the fixed PAPER_POSITION_SIZE_USD base with a bankroll-percentage formula so sizing compounds during winning runs and contracts during drawdowns. Simultaneously lower MAX_POSITION_SIZE_USD from $100 to $50. The confidence tier multiplier stays in place but all sizing is now bounded by the new $50 hard cap.
context: Current sizing uses a fixed $50 base regardless of bankroll balance, capped at $100. This means the bot risks the same dollar amount whether the bankroll is $300 or $1,500 — it does not compound wins and does not protect itself during drawdowns. The new formula replaces the fixed base with (STARTING_BANKROLL + realized_pnl) * RISK_PCT_PER_TRADE. The $100 cap is simultaneously dropped to $50 per operator instruction. Confidence tiers remain: they scale the base, but the $50 cap is the absolute ceiling.
do_not_touch: Any file not listed in allowed_files above

instructions: Add RISK_PCT_PER_TRADE=0.03 (3% of current bankroll per trade).
Add MIN_POSITION_USD=10 (floor so sizing does not collapse below $10 during deep drawdown).
Leave PAPER_POSITION_SIZE_USD in place for now — it will become the fallback default if bankroll cannot be read. Its value does not need to change.
Read the file in full before making any edits.
Locate the sizing block — currently: size_usd = clamp(base_usd * tier_mult, 0, MAX_POSITION_SIZE_USD) where base_usd = PAPER_POSITION_SIZE_USD.

Add three new env reads near the top of the file alongside the existing ones: RISK_PCT_PER_TRADE = float(os.getenv('RISK_PCT_PER_TRADE', '0.03')), MIN_POSITION_USD = float(os.getenv('MIN_POSITION_USD', '10')), STARTING_BANKROLL = float(os.getenv('STARTING_BANKROLL', '500')).

Replace the base_usd computation with bankroll-aware logic: import total_realized_pnl from core.db (it is already used elsewhere in the codebase — check if already imported before adding). Compute: current_bankroll = STARTING_BANKROLL + total_realized_pnl(). Compute: bankroll_base = max(MIN_POSITION_USD, current_bankroll * RISK_PCT_PER_TRADE). Then: size_usd = clamp(bankroll_base * tier_mult, MIN_POSITION_USD, MAX_POSITION_SIZE_USD).

The recommended_size_dollars override path (signal.components path) must still be respected: if signal.components['recommended_size_dollars'] is set, use that value clamped to MAX_POSITION_SIZE_USD as before — do not apply the bankroll formula in that case.
Add a log line at INFO level showing the computed sizing: log.info('SIZING bankroll=%.2f base=%.2f tier_mult=%.2f size_usd=%.2f', current_bankroll, bankroll_base, tier_mult, size_usd).
If total_realized_pnl() raises an exception, fall back to PAPER_POSITION_SIZE_USD * tier_mult and log a WARNING. Do not let a DB read failure prevent trade entry.

expected_steps: current_bankroll = 500 + 351.91 = 851.91  (note: use actual DB value, this is illustrative)

acceptance_criteria: RISK_PCT_PER_TRADE and MIN_POSITION_USD are present in .env
paper_exec.py reads current bankroll from STARTING_BANKROLL + total_realized_pnl()
sizing formula uses bankroll_base = max(MIN_POSITION_USD, bankroll * RISK_PCT_PER_TRADE)
confidence tier multiplier still applied to bankroll_base
recommended_size_dollars override path still respected and capped at 50
DB read failure falls back to PAPER_POSITION_SIZE_USD with WARNING log
INFO log line emitted on each sizing computation
verification: Read the updated sizing block in paper_exec.py and confirm formula matches spec
Read .env and confirm MAX_POSITION_SIZE_USD=50, RISK_PCT_PER_TRADE=0.03, MIN_POSITION_USD=10
Grep paper_exec.py for 'total_realized_pnl' — expect exactly one call site in sizing block
Grep paper_exec.py for 'recommended_size_dollars' — confirm override path still present
rollback: Revert core/paper_exec.py to prior version and restore MAX_POSITION_SIZE_USD=100 in .env. Remove RISK_PCT_PER_TRADE and MIN_POSITION_USD from .env. Restart bot.
restart_note: Bot must be restarted after this task is approved for new sizing to take effect.
deliverables: Exact lines changed in paper_exec.py with before/after
Worked example showing sizing output at current bankroll
